chapters.title: Introduction
chapters.description:
Hyperliquid is a sovereign Layer 1 blockchain purpose-built for on-chain finance — the first platform to run a fully on-chain Central Limit Order Book (CLOB) at institutional-grade throughput. Conceived by a founding team with roots in Hudson River Trading and quantitative finance, it was bootstrapped without venture capital and reached mainnet in early 2023.

Rather than forcing trading primitives onto a general-purpose chain, Hyperliquid stacks three specialized layers: HyperBFT for consensus, HyperCore for perpetual and spot matching, and HyperEVM for Ethereum-compatible smart contracts. All three share the same state and finalize in the same blocks — eliminating bridges, oracles and synchronization delays that typically plague multi-chain stacks.

The result is a venue that has already processed trillions in cumulative derivatives volume while remaining 100% on-chain, transparent and self-custodial — with the ambition to become the settlement layer for 24/7 global markets.

chapters.title: HyperBFT
chapters.description:
HyperBFT is Hyperliquid's custom Byzantine Fault Tolerant consensus, derived from the HotStuff protocol originally designed for Meta's Libra project. It is engineered around a single non-negotiable: deliver the latency needed for high-frequency trading without compromising the security guarantees institutions expect.

Four architectural choices make this possible. Pipelined consensus lets validators advance multiple block stages in parallel, collapsing median end-to-end latency to roughly 0.1–0.2 seconds. Optimistic execution runs transactions before finalization whenever network conditions allow. One-block finality makes every fill irreversible the moment it is included — there is no probabilistic settlement and no reorg risk. And classic BFT guarantees are preserved, tolerating up to one-third of malicious validators.

Block production is proportional to native HYPE staked, aligning validator economics with network security. Today the stack sustains ~200,000 orders per second with architectural headroom well beyond 1M OPS.

chapters.title: HyperCore
chapters.description:
HyperCore is Hyperliquid's financial execution engine — a Rust-based matching engine running fully on-chain, not a simulation or an off-chain sequencer. Every order, cancel, trade, liquidation and funding payment is recorded on the public ledger under the same HyperBFT consensus that secures the rest of the network.

At its heart is an on-chain Central Limit Order Book with price-time priority — the same primitive used by NYSE, NASDAQ and top CEXs, but settled natively on-chain. Execution is deterministic, so there is no black-box matching engine to trust. Trading operations (placement, cancellation, modification) are gas-free for end users: the protocol's economic model absorbs the cost, removing the friction that has historically handicapped on-chain trading.

A cross-margin system lets traders use their entire portfolio as collateral for perps and spot at once, and the engine currently handles ~200,000 orders per second with room to grow — enough to support market making, statistical arbitrage and delta-neutral strategies at institutional scale.

chapters.title: HyperEVM
chapters.description:
HyperEVM is Hyperliquid's general-purpose smart contract layer — Ethereum-compatible by design, so Solidity developers keep the tools, languages and mental models they already know. Critically, it is not a separate chain: HyperEVM and HyperCore are two execution environments secured by the same HyperBFT consensus, sharing a single state machine.

This unified architecture unlocks capabilities no L2 or rollup can match. Smart contracts can atomically read live HyperCore state — oracle prices, order book depth, positions, balances — and write back into it, all within a single block. No external oracle, no bridge, no cross-chain message queue.

To serve different workloads, HyperEVM runs a dual-block system: small blocks (2M gas, produced roughly every 2 seconds) for fast trades and near-instant confirmations, and big blocks (30M gas, ~once per minute) for heavy contract deployments. Permissionless builders can ship lending markets, vaults, options venues and structured products that plug straight into the native CLOB's liquidity.

chapters.title: HyperCore ↔ HyperEVM
chapters.description:
Because HyperCore and HyperEVM share the same state under the same consensus, interactions between them are atomic — a property that separates Hyperliquid from every multi-chain or appchain stack.

Two system interfaces expose this composability. Read Precompiles (starting at 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000800) let HyperEVM contracts query real-time HyperCore data — oracle prices, perpetual positions, spot balances, order book depth — with zero external dependency. No Chainlink, no API3, no pull-oracle latency.

CoreWriter (the system contract at 0x3333333333333333333333333333333333333333) is the write path: smart contracts can place limit orders, manage vault transfers, handle staking or send any HyperCore action using a versioned ABI. Actions are delayed by a few seconds to neutralize latency games and keep the venue fair for everyone.

The payoff for builders: fully composable DeFi backed by a real on-chain CLOB. Lending markets can liquidate directly on HyperCore, vaults can route trades without intermediaries, and structured products can settle against on-chain spot and perp liquidity in a single transaction.

chapters.title: $HYPE
chapters.description:
HYPE is the native token of Hyperliquid — the coordination asset that aligns validators, traders and builders around the network. It launched on November 29, 2024 through one of the largest community-first Genesis Drops in DeFi history, with no venture capital allocation and no pre-sale.

Its utilities sit at the base of the stack. HYPE is the gas token for HyperEVM, the staking asset that weights block production on HyperBFT, and the currency of core governance actions (HIP votes, validator parameters, stablecoin selection). Large-scale applications lock it: HIP-3 deployers must stake 500,000 HYPE to launch a permissionless perp market, and Aligned Quote Asset issuers (like USDH) stake 1,000,000 HYPE to earn protocol-level fee advantages.

Total supply is capped at 1 billion tokens, with ~334 million circulating and scheduled unlocks running through 2027 for contributors and the Hyper Foundation. The Assistance Fund — fed by a share of protocol fees — runs ongoing HYPE buybacks on the open market, tying token value accrual directly to real trading volume rather than inflationary emissions.

chapters.title: HIP Framework
chapters.description: Hyperliquid Improvement Proposals (HIPs) are the binding code upgrades that extend the protocol. Each HIP introduces a new primitive — token standards, liquidity bootstrapping, permissionless market creation — that the whole ecosystem then builds on.

subChapters.title: HIP-1
subChapters.subtitle: Native token standard
subChapters.description:
HIP-1 introduced Hyperliquid's foundational token standard, turning the platform from a perpetuals-only exchange into a generalized asset infrastructure. Any entity can deploy a native HIP-1 token permissionlessly by winning a 31-hour Dutch auction for a spot slot — a mechanism that prices market access fairly and prevents spam, with the auction fee paid in HYPE.

At deployment, a token's name, decimals and total supply are registered at the protocol level and distributed through a Genesis allocation to anchor holders. HIP-1 tokens can then be linked to ERC-20 contracts on HyperEVM, so the same asset flows seamlessly between the native CLOB and the smart contract layer without wrapping or bridging.

Integration with LayerZero, Chainlink CCIP and Wormhole extends HIP-1 tokens across the broader multi-chain ecosystem — but the canonical supply always lives on Hyperliquid, where the order book, the gas and the governance logic coexist.

subChapters.title: HIP-2
subChapters.subtitle: Hyperliquidity
subChapters.description:
HIP-2 solved the chronic cold-start problem for new token markets by embedding automated liquidity provision directly into the block transition logic — not as an operator-controlled vault, but as a consensus-secured market-making primitive called Hyperliquidity.

The strategy guarantees a 0.3% spread that refreshes every three seconds from the very first second a token is live, creating tradable liquidity without the need for a mercenary market maker. Because quotes are produced by the protocol itself, they cannot be withdrawn, rugged or selectively disabled.

Crucially, Hyperliquidity participates in the general-purpose order book rather than living in a segregated pool. That means sophisticated market makers can join alongside it as demand grows — a seamless hand-off from protocol-native bootstrapping to organic, competitive liquidity.

subChapters.title: HIP-3
subChapters.subtitle: Builder-deployed perpetuals
subChapters.description:
HIP-3, live since October 2025, is the most significant decentralization milestone in Hyperliquid's history — turning the platform from a curated exchange into permissionless perpetual-futures infrastructure.

Any builder that stakes 500,000 HYPE (~$15–20M at current valuations) can deploy a new perpetual market for any asset — crypto, equities, commodities, prediction markets — and capture 50% of the trading fees their market generates. The protocol supplies the matching engine, risk engine, margin system and liquidation flow; the builder supplies the oracle, the product thesis and the distribution. Validators can slash 20–100% of a deployer's stake for technical failures or oracle manipulation, and slashed HYPE is burned rather than redistributed.

Within three months of activation, HIP-3 markets crossed $1B in open interest and $25B+ in cumulative volume — with deployments already covering S&P 500, gold, silver and exotic assets. Combined with Growth Mode (a fee cut of 90%+ for bootstrapping new markets), HIP-3 positions Hyperliquid as a settlement rail any market operator can build on.
